item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: The image could not be reliably sourced and is thus assumed not compliant with CC 3.0. As such it has been removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Despite its length, this comes off as a pretty simple, solidly executed article for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I'm completely neutral on this. There are some word errors and grammar woes, but not enough to warrant a downvote. The effect is pretty cool, but not interesting enough for an upvote. And so…

Meh. Maybe it's worth keeping and maybe it isn't.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
This article is up for review. Check it out, we think it needs another look.

This article wavered for me. I wasn't hot on the description, but the interview was neat enough to neutral vote, and then the last note made me sigh and downvote. If more could be done with the imagery on display here, I would be happy. Maybe an additional couple of paragraphs in the description or summat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is quite the snazzy SCP, and though the SCP itself was good, personally the interview won me over. +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I understood the difficulty of location referred to the wormhole in Vietnam where the tank originally entered "limbo", and not to the one in the US where it came out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Lots of issues in the writing, if not also the tone, but I didn't downvote until I saw the untraceability of it. How can they even know about it if they can't find it? Doesn't it say where the field is located in the description?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like it, the best new SCP i've read for a while

item.title: Re: Fixes
item.content:encoded: Rewrite of this 'limbo space' done. Times altered. *cue dramatic music

item.title: Re: Fixes
item.content:encoded: I'm also hoping to flesh out the idea of a time/space issue, reinforcing the idea of a limbo space of many similarly-fated vehicles. I realise now it is little more than a concept…

item.title: Re: Fixes
item.content:encoded: The implication is that there are many such sites globally, but i'm unsure of how to get that across….

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Downvoted. The article doesn't hook me at all, the basic premise is either esoteric or not fleshed out very well, and the interview log (which makes up almost all of the article's meat) has a giant chunk of text.

It's better than a lot of the articles we've had recently, and I can see that you're trying, but I just don't think this article works well for me.

item.title: Re: Fixes
item.content:encoded: … I think I must've misread earlier. So these guys fell in a mudhold in Vietnam and came out in one of the lower 48 states? Huh… I'd say the hook needs a little more development before I commit to an upvote, personally. It's getting better, but it doesn't really grab me; at present all I'm seeing is vehicle-specific quicksand that one time spat out a tank that went missing in Vietnam. Does make me wonder what the other stuff that falls in goes to though…

item.title: Fixes
item.content:encoded: Sorted the random line break issue, revised containment procedures and description. Any criticism is still welcome

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There's something here, but it's too sparse for me to vote at the moment. I'll be watching this one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks for the tips, v2 is currently in the works, pending more comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Like it (being a history buff) but I think it may be over-weighted toward the interview. Also there's no containment listed for SCP-1973-1 or the crew (which would probably get their own SCP designation).
Also, "No anomalous activity reported in or by SCP-1973-1 or crew." is directly contradicted by the body of the article. It should at least qualify it as "No anomalous activity has been reported in or by SCP-1973-1 or crew since recovery."

Containment should describe procedures for vehicles coming out of SCP-1973 (which I presume SCP-1973-1 was only the first).

Also, it might up the weird factor if the tank crew as displaced in time as well as space (instead of having the interview in 1973, which is kind of on-the-nose, maybe have it in the 80s or 90s, or have it in the late 60s, and the tank is from 1973)

Anyway, the interview is good, but the article around it could use some fixing up.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ah yeah I've researched it now, it still looks good.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nope, not that at all. It's a WW(not sure which) animated feature in which the pilot (Porco Rosso) has the face of a pig after a relatively unexplained event that happened to him, he spends his days in dogfights while pursuing lost love. It's pretty decent with great production values (Ghibli studios).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: ok this film sounds great! Dead(?) pilots come back as animals? wow, must check it out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There is a scene very similar to this in the film. It happens with military aircraft, rather than vehicles. But, yeah, the parallels are quite eerie.

Only way it could have been closer is if they came back as some sort of anthropomorphic animals.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I used line breaks because it seemed a daunting block of text all in one, by the way. But thanks for the feedback i'll bear that in mind when I inevitably edit it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Porco Rosso? No I've never seen it, is it basically this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
So… it's quicksand but only on vehicles? And sometimes stuff that goes in ends up in another place entirely? Not quite feeling the hook here. Also, you've got some formatting errors. Mostly line-breaks where there shouldn't be. You should clean those up. Also, the description pretty much hits us out of nowhere with that information on SCP-1973-1; that should really be lead into, and the importance of 'Jeannie' (which I assume is the only vehicle we've recovered from SCP-1973?) should be established in the description. Keeping my vote neutral for now; I wanna see what this looks like when it's polished.

By the way…

Then it struck me what was really odd. No sound. There were like a million vehicles here, all going along steadily and not one of them made so much as a squeak. We saw the crews, sat there, facing forwards, unmoving. I thought I'd gone mad, or died, or both. We'd just got moving, the engine wasn't on, wasn't in gear or anything, we were just moving along. Jesus, we spent so long on that road. I don't know how long we had been on it when something happened, but it more than I care to imagine. Then yeah, as suddenly as we got there, we were in the air, rising above the convoy. We kept on going through the white, then black, then out.

*scratches head* Maybe I'm just reading too much into things, but did you watch Porco Rosso right before writing this?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This doesn't do much for me. But it's well-done, I imagine it will be other peoples' thing, and is pleasingly simple. A generous +1.
